title: Propagation Engine Rules (v1)
description: Canonical structural shape for the propagation engine's rule set. The engine at adapters/claude-code/hooks/propagation-trigger-router.sh loads a single rules file (build-doctrine/propagation/propagation-rules.json), evaluates each rule's trigger against the input event, runs the condition evaluator, dispatches the action on match, and writes an audit-log entry to build-doctrine/telemetry/propagation.jsonl regardless of match outcome (so the audit log captures both fired and unfired evaluations — the negative space is itself measurement data). Authored by Tranche 6a (2026-05-06) per the build-doctrine roadmap. Companion doc at build-doctrine/propagation/README.md.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Currently always 1. Validators accept current + N-1 for graceful migration.

metadata.description: Optional metadata describing the rule set as a whole (authoring, versioning, last-revision date).

rules.description: The rule set. Each rule is independently evaluated against every input event in the rule order; engine evaluates rules in the order they appear in the JSON array (lex-stable for the audit log).

id.description: Kebab-case unique identifier. Audit-log entries reference this for tuning. Conventionally prefixed with PT-N for trigger-taxonomy alignment (e.g., 'pt-3-adr-adoption-fanout', 'pt-6-findings-pattern').

description.description: One-paragraph human-readable description of what the rule does + when it fires. Required; substance review is the rule-author's discipline.

conjectural.description: True for rules whose thresholds or fan-out targets are hypotheses pending audit-log evidence (e.g., '≥3 similar findings within 7 days' for PT-6). The README's conjectural-rule disposition section documents how these get tuned.

pending_evidence.description: When conjectural is true, names the kind of evidence that would resolve the conjecture (e.g., 'audit-log-tuning', 'pilot-friction-notes', 'cross-project-comparison'). Optional; absent for proven rules.

severity.description: How loud the audit-log entry should be when this rule fires. 'info' = pure observation; 'warning' = surfaces in the next session-wrap summary; 'critical' = immediate stderr message.

owner.description: Logical role responsible for the rule's correctness (e.g., 'doctrine-curator', 'plan-lifecycle-maintainer'). Optional; informs which calibration roll-up surfaces drift.

trigger.description: Specifies which events the rule responds to. The engine matches an input event against a rule's trigger; non-matches skip to the next rule.

event_type.description: The kind of event the rule responds to. Engine matches input event's event_type against this string.

path_pattern.description: Optional glob or regex pattern restricting the trigger to specific paths (e.g., 'docs/decisions/NNN-*.md', 'build-doctrine/doctrine/*.md'). When absent, the rule matches every event of the named event_type.

metadata_match.description: Optional event-metadata constraints (e.g., {'status_to': 'COMPLETED'} for plan-status-flip rules).

condition.description: Optional gating logic evaluated AFTER trigger match but BEFORE action dispatch. Allows expressing 'fire when X AND Y' without proliferating event_type values.

type.description: 'always' (default): action fires whenever trigger matches. 'script': runs a bash script with event JSON on stdin; non-zero exit = condition fails, skip action. 'command': runs an inline bash command; same convention.

script_path.description: Required when type is 'script'. Path to the script that evaluates the condition. Receives the event JSON on stdin.

command.description: Required when type is 'command'. Bash command line; placeholders <event.path>, <event.metadata.X> substituted before exec.

timeout_ms.description: Per-condition timeout in ms. Default 100ms enforces the per-rule budget.

action.description: What the rule does when trigger matches AND condition succeeds.

type.description: 'log-only': no side effect; useful for measurement-only rules. 'script': runs a bash script with event JSON on stdin. 'command': runs an inline bash command. 'open-finding': appends an entry to docs/findings.md (action.finding_template required).

command.description: Required when type is 'command'. Bash command line.

finding_template.description: Required when type is 'open-finding'. Template for the finding entry; placeholders <event.path>, <event.metadata.X>, <rule.id> substituted before append.

timeout_ms.description: Per-action timeout in ms. Default 400ms; combined with condition's 100ms = 500ms per-rule total budget.
